mapping.a_element: the set of catalogue-based fields at many source positions whose joint statistics are modelled
mapping.b_element: the set of gauging stations on the river network whose joint statistics are modelled

mapping.a_element: estimating the dependence structure (Gaussian covariance) among the multiple observables
mapping.b_element: estimating the dependence structure (extremal dependence) among the multiple flow locations

brief_justification: Paper A estimates the disconnected/Gaussian (second-moment) covariance of angular power spectra using a Narrow-Kernel mode-coupling approximation plus Poisson self-pair noise, i.e. bulk Gaussian-field machinery; paper B instead models the joint tail via multivariate geometric extreme value theory, gauge functions and block-graph graphical models. Both merely estimate a joint dependence structure over many correlated quantities, but the governing machinery differs decisively (Gaussian power-spectrum covariance vs. extremal-dependence/EVT graphical models), so no role-for-role mapping of the actual mechanism holds.
